target: race, color, religion, sex, disability, familial status, national origin
7 classes: race, color, religion, sex, disability, familial status, national origin
all rental housing, sales, advertising, and financing
owner-occupied buildings with 4 or fewer units (Mrs. Murphy exemption)
civil penalties up to $16,000 for first violation, $65,000 for subsequent
households with children under 18, pregnant women, or those seeking custody
reasonable accommodations and modifications at landlord expense for common areas, tenant expense for interior
emotional support and service animals are not pets under FHA, no pet fees allowed
public accommodations and commercial facilities, NOT private residential rentals
applies to rental offices and common areas open to public, not individual units
landlords must disclose known lead-based paint for buildings built before 1978
EPA disclosure form must be signed by tenant before lease signing
EPA pamphlet 'Protect Your Family from Lead in Your Home' must be provided
up to $16,000 per violation per day for non-disclosure
buildings built after 1977 are exempt from lead paint disclosure requirements
no federal limit on security deposit amount, states set their own limits
landlords must maintain rental units in habitable condition regardless of lease terms
working plumbing, heating, electrical, safe structure, hot water, trash receptacles
proper written notice before eviction, court process required for removal
self-help evictions (changing locks, removing belongings, shutting off utilities)
tenant can sue for actual damages plus punitive damages in most states
landlord must get written consent before pulling tenant credit report
if denied based on credit report, landlord must provide adverse action notice with report source
tenant screening reports must be retained for minimum 5 years from date of report
